message:
test(storage): open-path regression for v5→v6 auto-migration of integer datetimes and bd 'done' status

End-to-end regression for the v6 migration + reader coercions landed
earlier this session. Opens a SqliteStorage against a TempDir, writes
a v5-shaped corruption (PRAGMA user_version=5 + mixed text/integer
datetime rows + a `status='done'` row), closes and reopens, and
asserts the reader returns the expected corrected state.

The test is the on-disk failure mode we observed in the wild:
- One row ("legacy-int") with a canonical RFC-3339 `created_at`, an
  integer-microseconds `updated_at` and `closed_at` matching
  `2026-04-20T02:44:48.000000Z`, and a close_reason of "done".
  This exercises the datetime storage-class normalization path.
- Another row ("legacy-done") carrying `status='done'` with no
  `closed_at`. This exercises the legacy Go-beads terminal-status
  alias mapping to `closed`.

After reopening (which triggers the migration), the test asserts:
- user_version is now 6 (migration ran exactly once).
- Both rows are readable without the previous
  `as_text().unwrap_or("")` silently mapping to UNIX_EPOCH.
- The integer-timestamp row's `updated_at` and `closed_at` are the
  RFC-3339 text the v6 migration writes via
  `strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%f', ...)`.
- The legacy-done row's status is now `Status::Closed` with a
  populated `closed_at` (COALESCE against updated_at/created_at).
- Reopening a second time is idempotent — the second-pass migration
  is a no-op and the values round-trip unchanged.

`.beads/issues.jsonl` — 4-line status touch from `br sync --flush-only`
following the migration regression landing plus the earlier 46y2 /
6s0p closes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
